meaning: adj.非现金的,无流动资金的
examples: This was pretty easy to do in an illiquid market.
It might be that the manager is buying illiquid assets.
Until recently gems have been considered an illiquid asset.
So, you sell your illiquid real estate and get, in return, another piece of illiquid real estate.
Preference shares are illiquid, or not easily traded, so it was easier to manipulate their prices.
